title: Centaurea scopulicola
taxonomy: Centaurea scopulicola is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Centaurea, which includes approximately 600 species of thistle and knapweed. The genus Centaurea is further classified within the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants that also includes chicory and artichokes. Centaurea scopulicola is native to the southwestern United States, where it grows in dry, sandy soils. This plant is often considered a rare and endangered species due to its limited range and habitat.

intro: Centaurea scopulicola, also known as the Rocky Mountain knapweed, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea scopulicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
